title: Quarto Quizdown Example
text:
A Quarto filter extension for writing short quizzes with a markdown-like syntax. It is based on the quizdown-js library and the hugo-quiz extension originally developed by bonartm:
Try out the original live code editor for quizdown: https://bonartm.github.io/quizdown-live-editor/
Here’s the README from the Github Repo, in case you missed it:

title: Quarto Quizdown Example
text:
Usage
Add the filter to your document front matter:
---
filters:
  - quizdown
---
Then write quizzes inside a ```quizdown code block:
```quizdown
---
shuffle_answers: true
---

## What is 2 + 2?

- [ ] 3
- [x] 4
    > Correct! Basic arithmetic.
- [ ] 5
```

title: Quarto Quizdown Example
text:
Question types







Type
Syntax




Single choice
Ordered list 1. [x]


Multiple choice
Unordered list - [x]


Sequence
Ordered list without checkboxes


Matching (fork addition)
- Left :: Right


Classification / multi-match (fork addition)
Same syntax — repeated right label auto-detected




Traditional matching (1-to-1)
Each right chip maps to exactly one left prompt. Chips disappear from the pool once placed.
```quizdown
## Match each animal to its sound!

- Dog :: Woof
    > Dogs bark to communicate.
- Cat :: Meow
    > Adult cats meow mainly with humans.
- Cow :: Moo
- :: Neigh
    > Horses neigh — a distractor here.
- :: Ribbit

> Think about farm and household animals.
```


Classification / multi-match
When the same right-side label appears more than once, it automatically becomes a reusable chip that stays in the pool. Perfect for classify-into-bins tasks.
```quizdown
## Classify each ML task as Regression or Classification!

- Predict house price :: Regression
    > The target (price) is a continuous number.
- Detect spam email :: Classification
    > The target is a category: spam or not spam.
- Forecast stock return :: Regression
    > Returns are continuous values, not categories.
- Identify handwritten digit :: Classification
    > Digits 0–9 are discrete categories.
- Estimate delivery time :: Regression
- :: Clustering
    > Clustering is unsupervised — no target label to predict.

> Regression → continuous target. Classification → categorical target.
```
Syntax rules for both modes:







Syntax
Meaning




- Left :: Right
Correct pair


- :: Right
Distractor chip (never correct for any prompt)


Indented > text under a pair
Per-pair feedback shown after evaluation


Indented > text under a distractor
Feedback shown when distractor is placed


Top-level > text
Overall question feedback / hint

title: Quarto Quizdown Example
text:
Print review mode
When a student prints the page (Cmd/Ctrl+P), the interactive widget is replaced with a static review sheet showing selected answers, correct answers, per-pair feedback, distractor feedback, and question-level status for all question types. No need to navigate to the results screen first.

title: Quarto Quizdown Example
text:
Attributions

Original Quarto extension: parmsam/quarto-quizdown (MIT, Sam Parmar 2025)
quizdown-js library: bonartm/quizdown-js (MIT, Malte Bonart 2020-21)

title: Matching — backward-compat & multi-match test
text:
This page tests both traditional 1-to-1 matching and the new multi-match (classification) mode in the same quiz, plus print review for both.
Press Cmd/Ctrl+P after answering to see the print review.

---
shuffle_answers: true
---

## Q1 (Traditional): Match each animal to its sound

Each right chip maps to exactly one left prompt, plus two distractors.

- Dog :: Woof
    > Dogs bark to communicate.
- Cat :: Meow
    > Adult cats meow mainly to communicate with humans.
- Cow :: Moo
    > Cows produce low-frequency moo calls.
- :: Neigh
    > Horses neigh — not used here.
- :: Ribbit
    > Frogs ribbit — not used here.

> Classic 1-to-1 matching with distractors.

## Q2 (Multi-match): Classify each ML task as Regression or Classification

Two reusable chips (Regression, Classification) plus one distractor (Clustering).

- Predict house price :: Regression
    > The target (price) is a continuous number.
- Detect spam email :: Classification
    > The target is a category: spam or not spam.
- Forecast stock return :: Regression
    > Returns are continuous values, not categories.
- Identify handwritten digit :: Classification
    > Digits 0–9 are discrete categories.
- Estimate delivery time :: Regression
    > Delivery time is a continuous quantity.
- :: Clustering
    > Clustering is unsupervised learning — there is no target label to predict.

> Regression → continuous target. Classification → categorical target.

title: quizdown for Quarto — MDS Extension
text: Interactive quizzes for Quarto documents, powered by quizdown-js. Fork of parmsam/quarto-quizdown, extended with new question types and a print review mode.

title: quizdown for Quarto — MDS Extension
text:
Install
quarto add UBC-MDS/quarto-quizdown-mds-ext
Then add the filter to your document front matter:
---
filters:
  - quizdown
---
Update an existing installation:
quarto update extension UBC-MDS/quarto-quizdown-mds-ext

title: quizdown for Quarto — MDS Extension
text:
Question types
All questions live inside a ```quizdown fenced code block. A single block can contain multiple questions — students navigate with the arrow buttons and submit all at once.

Single choice
Use a numbered list where exactly one item is marked [x]. Optionally add a > blockquote under any answer as per-option feedback.






NoteShow syntax





```quizdown
# What is 2 + 2?

1. [ ] 3
1. [x] 4
    > Correct — basic arithmetic!
1. [ ] 5
```




---
shuffle_answers: false
---

# What is the output of `len([1, 2, 3])`?

> `len()` returns the number of items in a list.

1. [ ] 1
1. [ ] 2
1. [x] 3
    > Correct — the list has 3 elements.
1. [ ] 4




Multiple choice
Use an unordered list (with -) where one or more items are marked [x].






NoteShow syntax





```quizdown
## Which are Python mutable types?

- [x] list
    > Lists can be modified after creation.
- [ ] tuple
    > Tuples are immutable.
- [x] dict
    > Dictionaries are mutable.
- [ ] str
    > Strings are immutable in Python.
```




---
shuffle_answers: false
---

## Which of the following are Python mutable types?

> A mutable object can be changed after it is created.

- [x] list
    > Lists can be modified after creation.
- [ ] tuple
    > Tuples are immutable — you cannot change their contents.
- [x] dict
    > Dictionaries are mutable — keys and values can be added or changed.
- [ ] str
    > Strings are immutable in Python.
- [x] set
    > Sets are mutable — you can add and remove elements.




Sequence
Use a numbered list without checkboxes. Students drag items into order.






NoteShow syntax





```quizdown
## Put the steps in order!

1. Step one
2. Step two
3. Step three
```




---
shuffle_answers: true
---

## Put the supervised ML workflow steps in order!

> A typical supervised ML project follows this sequence from problem definition to deployment.

1. Define the problem and success metric
2. Collect and clean data
3. Exploratory data analysis
4. Feature engineering
5. Split into train / validation / test sets
6. Train and tune a model
7. Evaluate on the test set
8. Deploy and monitor




Matching (1-to-1)
Use an unordered list with Left :: Right pairs. Each right chip belongs to exactly one left prompt. Add a distractor with :: Value (no left side). Per-pair feedback uses an indented > blockquote.






NoteShow syntax





```quizdown
## Match each term to its definition!

- Term A :: Definition A
    > Feedback for this pair.
- Term B :: Definition B
- :: Red herring
    > Feedback shown when this distractor is placed.

> Overall question hint.
```




---
shuffle_answers: true
---

## Match each Python data structure to its key property!

- list :: Ordered, mutable sequence
    > Lists maintain insertion order and can be changed after creation.
- tuple :: Ordered, immutable sequence
    > Tuples are like lists but cannot be modified once created.
- dict :: Key-value store with unique keys
    > Dictionaries map unique keys to values; order is preserved in Python 3.7+.
- set :: Unordered collection of unique values
    > Sets automatically deduplicate — great for membership tests.
- :: Linked list
    > Python has no built-in linked list (though `collections.deque` is close).

> Think about mutability and ordering.




Matching (multi-match / classification) (MDS addition)
When the same right-side label appears for multiple left items, it automatically becomes a reusable chip that stays in the pool after being placed — perfect for classify-into-bins tasks.






NoteShow syntax





```quizdown
## Classify each task!

- Task A :: Category 1
    > Feedback for Task A.
- Task B :: Category 2
    > Feedback for Task B.
- Task C :: Category 1
    > Feedback for Task C — same chip as Task A.
- :: Red herring
    > This option is never correct.
```




---
shuffle_answers: true
---

## Classify each ML task as Regression or Classification!

> Regression predicts a **continuous** value; Classification predicts a **category**.

- Predict house price :: Regression
    > The target (price) is a continuous number.
- Detect spam email :: Classification
    > The output is a category: spam or not spam.
- Forecast stock return :: Regression
    > Returns are continuous values, not categories.
- Identify handwritten digit :: Classification
    > Digits 0–9 are discrete categories.
- Estimate delivery time :: Regression
    > Delivery time is a continuous quantity.
- Diagnose disease from image :: Classification
    > Disease/no-disease is a categorical outcome.
- :: Clustering
    > Clustering is unsupervised — there is no target label to predict.

title: quizdown for Quarto — MDS Extension
text:
Combined quiz — all types together
A single quiz block with all five question types, showing how they work end-to-end including the print review (try Cmd/Ctrl+P after evaluating).






Tip



Print review: after answering all questions and pressing ✓✓ (evaluate), press Cmd/Ctrl+P. The print dialog will show a static review sheet with your selections, correct answers, and feedback for every question.



---
shuffle_answers: true
---

# Single choice: Python indexing

What is the value of `x[2]` after this code?

~~~python
x = [2, 3, 4]
x[2] = 9
print(x[2])
~~~

1. [x] 9
    > Correct — `x[2] = 9` overwrites index 2.
1. [ ] 4
    > That was the original value before the assignment.
1. [ ] 2
1. [ ] 3

## Multiple choice: supervised learning tasks

Select **all** that apply.

> Supervised learning requires labeled training data.

- [x] Predicting house prices
    > Yes — continuous labeled target.
- [ ] Clustering customers
    > No — clustering is unsupervised.
- [x] Classifying email as spam
    > Yes — categorical labeled target.
- [x] Forecasting temperature
    > Yes — continuous labeled target.
- [ ] Finding hidden topics in documents
    > No — topic modelling is unsupervised.

## Sequence: ML workflow

> A typical supervised ML project follows this sequence.

1. Define the problem and success metric
2. Collect and clean data
3. Exploratory data analysis
4. Feature engineering
5. Train and tune a model
6. Evaluate on the test set
7. Deploy and monitor

## Matching (1-to-1): animals and sounds

- Dog :: Woof
    > Dogs bark to communicate.
- Cat :: Meow
    > Adult cats mostly meow to communicate with humans.
- Cow :: Moo
    > Cows vocalize with low-frequency moo calls.
- :: Neigh
    > Horses neigh — a distractor here.
- :: Ribbit
    > Frogs ribbit — a distractor here.

> Think about farm and household animals.

## Matching (multi-match): classify ML tasks

Chips **Regression** and **Classification** are reusable.

- Predict house price :: Regression
    > The target (price) is a continuous number.
- Detect spam email :: Classification
    > The target is a category: spam or not spam.
- Forecast stock return :: Regression
    > Returns are continuous values, not categories.
- Identify handwritten digit :: Classification
    > Digits 0–9 are discrete categories.
- Estimate delivery time :: Regression
    > Delivery time is a continuous quantity.
- :: Clustering
    > Clustering is unsupervised — no target label.

> Regression → continuous. Classification → categorical.

title: quizdown for Quarto — MDS Extension
text:
Attributions

Original Quarto extension: parmsam/quarto-quizdown (MIT, Sam Parmar 2025)
quizdown-js library: bonartm/quizdown-js (MIT, Malte Bonart 2020–21)
